jokes.text: I Googled that bug. It's some sort of demonic cricket that 'takes chunks out of your skin when provoked.' Chunks is the word that Google used.
jokes.setup: Character explains researching a bug online
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity of 'demonic cricket' and emphasis on 'chunks'
jokes.explanation: Great specificity with 'demonic cricket' and 'chunks' - the repetition of Google's word choice adds absurdity. Character-specific neurosis.

jokes.text: I have this irrational fear they are gonna crawl into my mouth and nest at night
jokes.setup: Explaining bug phobia
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disturbing imagery of bugs nesting in mouth
jokes.explanation: Effectively gross and specific fear, fits Stevie's deadpan neurotic character well.

jokes.text: Not with your mouth open anyway.
jokes.setup: Stevie says she can't sleep in the room with the bug
jokes.punchline: David's cruel callback to her mouth-nesting fear
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that's both cruel and witty. Maximum economy - four words that land perfectly. Classic David Rose snark.

jokes.text: That's not a nice thing to say to me.
jokes.setup: David's cruel mouth comment
jokes.punchline: Stevie's understated, wounded response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie understatement - wounded but delivered flatly. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Hey! Where's my towel?! John? Johnny?! Where's my towel?!
jokes.setup: Shower turning off, expectation of normal post-shower routine
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic escalation from shower to full panic over missing towel
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira making a minor inconvenience into high drama. The escalation from 'John' to 'Johnny' adds character specificity.

jokes.text: He had a boner!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining euphemism 'pitching a tent'
jokes.punchline: Blunt clarification destroys any subtlety
jokes.explanation: Typical Jocelyn - missing social cues and being inappropriately direct. Functional character comedy.

jokes.text: That's not dirt. I used it to kill a bug.
jokes.setup: Moira complaining about dirty towel
jokes.punchline: Johnny's matter-of-fact revelation connects to opening bug storyline
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure and Johnny's oblivious delivery makes it funnier. Sets up Moira's scream perfectly.

jokes.text: (Moira screams in anguish)
jokes.setup: Learning the towel was used to kill a bug
jokes.punchline: Moira's over-the-top horror reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira reaction - turns minor inconvenience into Greek tragedy. Great physical comedy beat.

jokes.text: Let's not and say we did.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn suggesting spa trip
jokes.punchline: Moira's dismissive twist on common phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of 'let's say we did and not do it' - very Moira sophisticated dismissal with perfect economy.

jokes.text: So now I'm fat and stressed?
jokes.setup: Johnny mentioning 'weight she's carrying'
jokes.punchline: Moira misinterpreting emotional weight as physical weight
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke, and Moira's vanity makes the misinterpretation character-specific and believable.

jokes.text: or I will strip out of this robe and I will air dry in the street!
jokes.setup: Demanding Johnny find a towel
jokes.punchline: Absurd threat to air dry naked in public
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Moira escalation. 'Air dry in the street' is wonderfully specific and ridiculous.

jokes.text: No, Meadow Harvest is exactly how it sounds. It just changes everyday.
jokes.setup: David asking what's in the Meadow Harvest smoothie
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that explains nothing while claiming to explain everything
jokes.explanation: Great example of small-town logic that makes no sense. Twyla's sweet confusion is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Yes, it is. Yup. At times. And then other times, not.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to clarify if the smoothie is fruity
jokes.punchline: Twyla's completely unhelpful circular logic
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity of Twyla's non-explanations perfectly

jokes.text: Run through you like Niagara Falls.
jokes.setup: Warning about Twyla's smoothie effects
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor for digestive consequences
jokes.explanation: Vivid, memorable metaphor delivered with perfect timing. Good introduction to Ted's character - helpful but blunt.

jokes.text: Um, I usually prescribe them to my patients when they're feeling a little... backed up.
jokes.setup: Explaining smoothie effects
jokes.punchline: Revelation he actually prescribes them medically
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - takes the joke from warning to professional medical practice. Perfect character introduction for Ted.

jokes.text: Oh! It's basically the same thing.
jokes.setup: Ted revealing he's a veterinarian
jokes.punchline: Alexis dismissing the difference between human and animal medicine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis obliviousness - casually insulting while trying to be supportive. Great character moment.

jokes.text: I'm sure there's like another girl... somewhere.
jokes.setup: Ted saying she's the most beautiful girl the town's ever seen
jokes.punchline: Faux-modest response that's actually supremely confident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis fake modesty - pretending humility while clearly believing she's the most beautiful. The pause and 'somewhere' sells it.

jokes.text: (Alexis giggles and spits out smoothie)
jokes.setup: Trying Twyla's infamous smoothie after Ted's warnings
jokes.punchline: Physical confirmation of Ted's digestive warnings
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical payoff to Ted's warnings. Visual comedy that confirms the setup without words.

jokes.text: I do Coachella every year, so...
jokes.setup: Stevie saying he's 'at one with nature'
jokes.punchline: David's idea of nature is a luxury music festival
jokes.explanation: Perfect David Rose logic - equating a luxury festival with nature. Reveals character's privileged worldview in four words plus 'so...'

jokes.text: High thread count sheets? Colognes that smell like fireplace...
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting hunting isn't his thing
jokes.punchline: David's defensive listing of his actual interests
jokes.explanation: Great character specificity - 'colognes that smell like fireplace' is perfectly David. Self-aware while being defensive.

jokes.text: So... is this a tweed or a camo situation?
jokes.setup: Agreeing to go hunting
jokes.punchline: David's concern is entirely about fashion choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's priorities remain fashion even when agreeing to hunt. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Go. To. Hell! You're off... you're a fool!
jokes.setup: Struggling with bedding setup
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic fury at inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira treating household tasks as personal affronts. The escalation from 'hell' to 'fool' is perfectly theatrical.

jokes.text: Really mean?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn saying salon gives 'mean scalp massage'
jokes.punchline: Moira taking 'mean' literally rather than as 'good'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay misunderstanding. Moira's confusion about colloquial language fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: (Awkward silence after David mentions plans)
jokes.setup: Stevie sharing her evening plans
jokes.punchline: David not reciprocating the social exchange
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy beat. David's social obliviousness creates natural awkwardness that Stevie has to address.

jokes.text: Money.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking what she looks for in a husband
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt, one-word assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery. One word that cuts through Alexis's romantic delusions with brutal honesty. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Did you know Richard Gere's middle name is Tiffany?
jokes.setup: Awkward pause in conversation
jokes.punchline: Alexis filling silence with random celebrity trivia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis behavior - deflecting uncomfortable moments with random celebrity facts. Absurd but character-consistent.

jokes.text: I went on a blind date to Bali with Leo, so... I'm pretty sure I'm gonna be fine.
jokes.setup: David warning about traveling with strangers
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual mention of international blind date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis past - casually mentioning something that sounds completely insane. 'Leo' suggests Leonardo DiCaprio without saying it.

jokes.text: Because he knows how much I love it when mom and dad interrogate your boyfriends.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why Ted is talking to their parents
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic explanation of family dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic joke. David's sarcasm about parental interference feels authentic and relatable.

jokes.text: Oh... alcoholism. / Tsk, tsk. Affair. Poor thing.
jokes.setup: Ted explaining his parents' amicable divorce
jokes.punchline: Moira and Johnny assuming worst-case scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalating assumptions. The parents can't accept that divorce could be civil, revealing their own cynical worldview.

jokes.text: Don't even think about it. Ain't gonna happen. Ted, not on the first date.
jokes.setup: Alexis joking about staying out all night
jokes.punchline: Johnny's mortifying parental overshare about sexual expectations
jokes.explanation: Classic embarrassing dad moment. The direct address to Ted makes it more cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: So Antonio's buffet, huh? So many choices, one little plate.
jokes.setup: Learning about the dinner date location
jokes.punchline: Johnny's subtle dig at the restaurant quality
jokes.explanation: Good dad humor - passive-aggressive concern about the date venue

jokes.text: You and your nightgown can tippy-toe back to bed. I'm late.
jokes.setup: Johnny's continued fretting about hunting
jokes.punchline: David's dismissive description of his father's appearance
jokes.explanation: 'Tippy-toe' is perfectly condescending David language. Creates funny visual of Johnny's concerned hovering.

jokes.text: Turkey virgin!
jokes.setup: David admitting he's never killed before
jokes.punchline: Roland's inappropriate and loud announcement
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland inappropriateness. 'Virgin' reference is crude but fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Does this come in a slimmer cut?
jokes.setup: Being given hunting coveralls
jokes.punchline: David treating utilitarian gear like fashion
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - can't turn off his fashion consciousness even in hunting gear. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I actually introduced him to Beyonce on the car ride on the way home, so that was very special.
jokes.setup: Describing her date with Ted
jokes.punchline: Treating playing Beyonce music like a personal introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis narcissism - treating playing music like she's personally connected to Beyonce. Reveals her delusional self-importance.

jokes.text: Well, he did that too.
jokes.setup: Stevie saying kiss on cheek is like handshake
jokes.punchline: Revealing Ted actually did shake her hand too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the date seem even more formal/awkward. Deadpan delivery sells the cringe.

jokes.text: So how are your bowels? Good and tight?
jokes.setup: Starting conversation before hunt
jokes.punchline: Roland's inappropriate opening question
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland inappropriate overshare. 'Good and tight' makes it even more uncomfortable and specific.

jokes.text: You know, delicate. Fragile. Scrimpy.
jokes.setup: Describing David as indoorsy
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults ending with made-up word 'scrimpy'
jokes.explanation: 'Scrimpy' is a wonderful made-up word that sounds like it should be real. Good escalating insult structure.

jokes.text: Have to admit, not into babies so it's kind of a guessing game for me.
jokes.setup: Being told to treat gun like newborn baby
jokes.punchline: David's honest admission about babies
jokes.explanation: Perfect David honesty that's both relatable and reveals character. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I wanna say Tanya Harding.
jokes.setup: Trying to identify who Moira looks like
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to disgraced figure skater
jokes.explanation: Great specific reference that's both visually accurate and hilariously unflattering. Johnny's tentative delivery sells it.

jokes.text: No, it's a dead possum. Yes, it's a wig, John.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking if it's her wig
jokes.punchline: Moira's sarcastic escalation to dead animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira sarcasm - 'dead possum' is both absurd and actually describes how the wig looks. Great delivery setup.

jokes.text: But not necessarily. / Sad?! You think this is a sad look?
jokes.setup: Moira trying to save face about wig
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's genuine offense at the implication
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation as Moira's attempt at tact backfires. Jocelyn's hurt reaction makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I'm stuck looking like every other inmate in this godforsaken prison.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she's wearing the wig
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic comparison of the town to a prison
jokes.explanation: Good Moira melodrama with the prison metaphor being appropriately dramatic

jokes.text: I feel like one of the Manson girls.
jokes.setup: David's reaction after shooting turkey
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to cult murderers
jokes.explanation: Dark, unexpected reference that's both shocking and perfectly David. The specificity of 'Manson girls' vs just 'murderer' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Even tougher when you shoot it in the neck 'cause then it takes longer to die.
jokes.setup: David feeling bad about shooting
jokes.punchline: Roland's matter-of-fact explanation making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good Roland moment - making David feel worse with unnecessary detail. Dark humor that fits his insensitive character.

jokes.text: You have a look that says, 'Hi. I'm Jocelyn, and I know who I am!'
jokes.setup: Moira trying to compliment Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about knowing her identity
jokes.explanation: Good Moira attempt at kindness that reveals her own identity crisis. The specific phrasing makes it sound both sincere and condescending.

jokes.text: I don't who I am from one moment to the next.
jokes.setup: Explaining her identity struggles
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Moira self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Rare vulnerable Moira moment that's both funny and touching. Explains her wig obsession in character terms.

jokes.text: But if you ever... Ever catch me shopping at the Blouse Barn, you must shoot me in the temple.
jokes.setup: Talking about adapting to small town life
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic threat about shopping at discount store
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira escalation - treating discount shopping as death-worthy offense. 'In the temple' is specific and dramatic.

jokes.text: My son is hunting right now with your husband, so I... I know you people have guns.
jokes.setup: Asking Jocelyn to promise to shoot her
jokes.punchline: Logical explanation of how Jocelyn would access weapons
jokes.explanation: Good callback to hunting plot. 'You people' adds subtle class distinction humor. Moira's practical approach to her dramatic request.

jokes.text: 'cause usually it's more fun. Usually Ray comes and... gets wasted and... Spends the whole day singing Carole King ballads.
jokes.setup: David asking why hunting is appealing
jokes.punchline: Bizarre tradition of drunk Carole King performances
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail about hunting tradition. 'Carole King ballads' is specific enough to be believable while being ridiculous.

jokes.text: Jocelyn! You look like Jocelyn!
jokes.setup: Seeing Moira's wig transformation
jokes.punchline: David pointing out the obvious resemblance
jokes.explanation: Good callback moment but fairly obvious observation. David's delivery and timing make it work better than the concept alone.

jokes.text: It was an indelible image that I had scarred in my mind until I saw what was... Happening up there with you.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's traumatized from hunting
jokes.punchline: Moira's appearance being worse than killing a turkey
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - comparing Moira's wig to traumatic turkey killing. Good callback structure with perfect David snark.
